criteria.criterion: Winning entry into `in_progress` sets compiled `plan_review_phase=execution`.
Winning entry into `open` sets compiled `plan_review_phase=planning`.

citation.description: _fold_plan_review_phase only mutates state for in_progress/open targets; all others leave plan_review_phase unchanged.

criteria.criterion: Other winning status targets preserve the projection.

citation.description: _fold_plan_review_phase is called on winning branch (lines 275, 303) but not in the losing-fork else at lines 279-283.

criteria.criterion: Losing fork STATUS events never update the projection.

citation.description: Force=True status passes through process_status normally; the force field is data-only and does not change the phase projection path.

criteria.criterion: Force transitions follow the same winning target rule.

citation.description: _snapshot_strip_keys() returns only {updated_at, signature}; plan_review_phase is not stripped, confirmed by test at test_plan_review_phase_reducer.py:98.

criteria.criterion: Normal SNAPSHOT compaction and older-reducer snapshot pass-through preserve the exact field value.

citation.description: test_legacy_snapshot_bootstraps_phase_without_mutating_events and test_phase_bearing_snapshot_preserves_historical_value cover the forward-upgrade reboot from compiled status.

criteria.criterion: The rollback test proves old-code raw-event re-reduction drops the unknown field and forward upgrade reboots it from compiled status, with the documented precision-loss window.
Pre-feature open/idea snapshots bootstrap planning once; every other known status bootstraps execution once.

citation.description: Structured INFO log with event, ticket_id, compiled_status, phase fields. No write path invoked.

criteria.criterion: Bootstrap emits the exact structured INFO and does not append ticket events.

citation.description: _REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION bumped to 7 with comment 'replay projects plan_review_phase and bootstraps pre-feature snapshots'.

criteria.criterion: Reducer-cache versioning activates the lazy bootstrap idempotently.

citation.description: build_manifest inserts review-phase: planning immediately after advisory: line, before rebar-version: line.

criteria.criterion: Planning manifests contain exactly `review-phase: planning` in the documented position.

citation.description: priority-floor line is conditionally appended for execution phase with :.2f format = '0.80'.

criteria.criterion: Execution manifests contain exactly `review-phase: execution` then `priority-floor: 0.80` in the documented position.

citation.description: When absent=True (no phase/floor tokens), validate_review_phase_metadata returns {phase: planning, priority_floor: None}.

criteria.criterion: Legacy phase-less attestations parse as planning.

citation.description: validate_review_phase_metadata raises ManifestFormatError for all named error cases: unknown phase, bool/non-numeric floor, out-of-range, execution without floor, floor without phase, etc.

criteria.criterion: Every named phase/floor syntax error raises `ManifestFormatError`; existing manifest parsers remain unchanged.

citation.description: review_phase_status: current_phase='planning' + signed_phase='execution' returns 'incompatible'.

criteria.criterion: Planning state accepts planning/legacy and rejects syntactically valid execution attestations.

citation.description: review_phase_status: current='execution', signed='planning' → compatible; current='execution', signed='execution', floor >= 0.80 → compatible (float(floor) < 0.80 is False).

criteria.criterion: Execution state accepts planning/legacy and execution floor >= 0.80.

citation.description: float(floor) < 0.80 → returns 'incompatible'.

criteria.criterion: Execution state rejects a syntactically valid execution floor < 0.80 as incompatible.

citation.description: ManifestFormatError caught → plan_health['phase_status'] = 'malformed'.

criteria.criterion: Malformed phase/floor produces `phase_status=malformed`.

citation.description: DerivedPlanReviewHealth TypedDict with required phase_status field; compute_validity sets it at lines 519-523 for all plan-review paths.

criteria.criterion: `DerivedPlanReviewHealth.phase_status` is present on every plan-review validity result.

citation.description: compute_validity parses phase/floor and sets phase_status on every plan-review kind call. resign.py calls sidecar.parse_review_phase_metadata and review_phase_status.

criteria.criterion: Claim, reuse, drift refresh, and re-sign enforce phase through the shared validity/phase helpers; child 2bbd and cb6b consume the same boundary later.

citation.description: phase/floor parsing only in the `if kind == _MANIFEST_PREFIX:` block (lines 486-538); completion-verifier has a separate branch at lines 555-577. Tested by test_completion_validity_never_reads_plan_phase.

criteria.criterion: Completion-verifier validity never calls phase, floor, or pin parsers.
Re-sign parses the exact v2 JSON phase fields through `sidecar.parse_review_phase_metadata`, uses the recorded values, and refuses every incompatible/malformed variant.

citation.description: Local _threshold_for closure: if execution_review and blocking_enabled: block_threshold = max(block_threshold, 0.80). Returns original pair otherwise.

criteria.criterion: Execution Pass-3 destructures `(block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` and floors only the float threshold when blocking is enabled.
A production workflow integration test starts from compiled execution phase and proves both YAML decide branches receive `review_phase` and block a finding below 0.80 that planning would not block.
The verify-failure recovery test proves `gate_dispatch` passes execution phase to Pass-3 and legacy missing phase defaults planning.
Schema golden tests prove `review_phase` is required in both schemas, precheck remains closed, decide input remains open, and schema/YAML/code rollback is version-coherent.

citation.description: When execution_review is False (planning), returns original (block_threshold, blocking_enabled) unchanged.

criteria.criterion: Planning Pass-3 returns the original threshold pair unchanged.

citation.description: test_execution_review_does_not_enable_advisory_only_criterion: blocking_enabled=False is not changed by execution_review.

criteria.criterion: Advisory-only criteria, novelty/completion floors, deterministic failures, and visible sub-floor findings retain their documented behavior.
A stable generation equal to the initial generation signs and commits under one lock.

citation.description: fresh != initial_generation → PlanReviewGenerationChanged (terminal stale). Dataclass equality covers all fields including phase, own_material, relation_snapshot.

criteria.criterion: Any stable phase, own-material, relationship, related-fingerprint, or generation-field change returns terminal unsigned stale.

citation.description: MAX_GENERATION_ATTEMPTS=3; WARN logged with attempt/before/after; exhaustion raises PlanReviewGenerationRetryable.

criteria.criterion: HEAD instability retries at most three times with exact WARN fields, then returns retryable unsigned.
Under-lock mismatch retries; `LockTimeout` returns retryable unsigned; neither writes a signature.

citation.description: Other exceptions: _log ERROR plan_review_sign_aborted, raise PlanReviewGenerationError.

criteria.criterion: Dirty/unmerged or terminal signing failure returns unsigned and emits `plan_review_sign_aborted`.

citation.description: LLMUnavailableError caught before LLM runs → _degraded_plan_review_verdict (INDETERMINATE, no sign). initial_generation collected before LLM in __init__.py:558.

criteria.criterion: Model/provider failure before signing writes no ticket event or signature and retains existing unsigned INDETERMINATE behavior.
The production-path integration test covers stable collection, under-lock HEAD re-verification, signature append, and commit end to end.

citation.description: All five required sections present: '### Phase/floor manifest contract', '### `phase_status` compatibility', '### Why the execution floor is fixed at 0.80', '### `PlanReviewGeneration` signing transaction', '### Phase rollback and precision loss'. Verified by test_plan_review_docs.py.

criteria.criterion: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains sections for the phase/floor manifest contract, phase_status compatibility table, fixed-0.80 rationale, PlanReviewGeneration transaction, and rollback precision-loss behavior.
